lines.text: Coffee place, Joey is there, Chandler is entering

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey. So where's Mon?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, she's at home, putting up decorations for Rachel's birthday party tonight.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And you're not helping?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I tried, but apparantly singing "I will survive" in a helium voice - not helping.
Ross and Rachel enter with Emma in Rachel's hands

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey you guys!

lines.speaker: Joey and Chandler
lines.text: (start to sing) Hey! Happy birthday ...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Shhh don't say that loud, Gunther's gonna want to hug me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, good news everyone, we finally found a nanny. This is Molly (points to Molly). Molly, Chandler, Joey.

lines.speaker: Joey and Chandler
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Molly
lines.text: Hi.
Emma starts to cry

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ooh, somebody's getting a little fussy.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You damn right I am, I've been waiting for a cookie for 7 minutes.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ok, you know what, I'm just gonna take her outside.

lines.speaker: Molly
lines.text: No, you stay, I'll do it (takes Emma from Rachel).

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: OK, thank you.

lines.speaker: Molly
lines.text: Nice to meet you guys (to Chandler and Joey).

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, you too.
Molly leaves

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, wow, Molly is just great!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, Bravo on the hot nanny.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What? You really think she's hot?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Are you kidding? If I wasn't married she'd be rejecting me right now.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And Joey?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: How do you think she's doing?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Am I the only one who doesn't think that she's hot? Ross?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Eh, I mean, I mena she's not unattractive but hot? I ....

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thank you! (goes to get coffee)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Now that Rachel's gone?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So hot I cried myself to sleep last night. (Joey and Chandler clap their hands)
OPENING CREDITS
Mike and Phoebe in Phoebe's place, Phoebe is doing a crossword puzzle

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey Mike, what's the capital of Peru?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Lima.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No. It starts with a "v" and ends with an "x". Helpfully with a "to" in the middle.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: You know, kinda think of it, the capital of Peru IS "vtox". (opens the kitchen cabinet) Oh god! Oh!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: OK, I don't want to freak you out or anything, but I think I just saw a rat in your cupboard.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (relived) Oh, whew, no, that's Bob.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: What, is he your pet rat?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well not so much a pet as, you know, an occasional visitor who I put food out for, you know. Kinda like Santa. Except Santa doesn't poop on the plate of cookies.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: You can't keep a rat in your appartment! They're extremely unsanitary, and they transmit leptospirosis and hantavirus.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What are those?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: I don't know but they don't sound like spa treatments. You have to get rid of it!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: OK, fine, if it means that much to you I'll get rid of Bob.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So weird, you think he's so gross and you're willing to eat his crackers. (Mike throws out everything in his mouth)
Rachel's office, Rachel comes in and Gavin is there

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Gavin
lines.text: Hello

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Gavin, I just wanted to say thank you again for watching Emma yesterday during the presentation. I really owe you an appology.

lines.speaker: Gavin
lines.text: For what?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, when we first met, you know, I thought you were pompous and arrogant and obnoxious ...

lines.speaker: Gavin
lines.text: Is this your first appology?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, I just mean that, you know, first impressions don't mean anything. And I-I think you're a really good guy and I'm sorry that I misjudged you.
Heather walks in

lines.speaker: Heather
lines.text: Good morning!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hello. (to Gavin) But you know what, hey, new day, new leaf, I am just really really happy ... (sees Gavin staring at Heather) I'm sorry, obviously Heather's ass has something more important to say so I'll just wait 'till it's finished.

lines.speaker: Gavin
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I was giving you an appology and you were totally checking her out!

lines.speaker: Gavin
lines.text: I wasn't checking her out. I'm in fashion, I was looking at her skirt. Or was it pants? I didn't really see what happened below the ass area.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh wow, you are really, you're really a creep.

lines.speaker: Gavin
lines.text: Why do you even care if I was looking at her? Are you jealous?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah, I'm jealous. "Oh Gavin, please, please look at my ass". (Gavin starts looking) Stop looking at my ass! I mean, I just think you are totally inappropriate, ok? This is a work environment, she's your subordinate.

lines.speaker: Gavin
lines.text: I thought it was ok when you slept with your old assistant Tag.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That is totally different for two reasons. One - I didn't know that you knew that. And two, I wasn't some creep staring at his ass, we had a deap meaningful relationship.

lines.speaker: Gavin
lines.text: Huh. What's Tag's last name?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It was ... (can't remember) oh my god. He didn't have a last name. It was just "Tag". You know, like Cher, or, you know, Moses.

lines.speaker: Gavin
lines.text: But it was a deap meaningful relationship.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, you know what - my first impression of you was absolutely right. You are arrogant, you are pompous ... Morgan! Morgan! Tag's last name was Morgan! Huh!

lines.speaker: Gavin
lines.text: It was Jones.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah well what are you, his boyfriend?
Coffee place, Molly holding Emma and talking to Joey

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Man, I wish I had a nanny like you.

lines.speaker: Molly
lines.text: You mean, when you were a baby.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Sure.
Chandler is staring at Molly

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Would you stop staring at her?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I wasn't staring. I was leering.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What's the big deal with her? Maybe she's attractive in an obvious kind of way.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, obvious beauty's the worst. You know, when it's right there in your face. Me, I like to have to work to find someone attractive. Makes me feel like I earned it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Looks like Joey is doing allright with her.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah. Hey, that was nice of you guys to back off and let Joey get the girl for once.

lines.speaker: Molly
lines.text: (to Ross) I'm gonna take her back to the appartment.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ok, I'll be home right after work. Ok, by Emma-Wemma-Demma, I love you - wovyou dovyou ...

lines.speaker: Molly
lines.text: Bye

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: They've elected me to talk to you about the baby talk - it's not so good.

lines.speaker: Molly
lines.text: I think it's sweet. (goes to leave)

lines.speaker: Ross, Joey and Chandler
lines.text: Bye, Emma-Wemma-Demma.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (to Joey) Hey, listen, Joey, about Molly, I really prefer if you didn't go after her.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Because it took us months to find a good nanny and I wouldn't want anything to, you know, drive her away.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So, what, you think I'm just gonna sleep with her and never call her again and things are gonna get uncomfortable? (thinks about it) Yeah, sounds about right.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come on, there are plenty of other women out thereok? Just - just forget about her, ok? Just, she's off limits.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, man, why did have to go and say that for? Now that you told me I can't have it makes me want her even more!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What are you, a child?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yes!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Joey, come on now, for me! Please, just-just try to focus your sexual energy on someone else.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Fine. (looks around, then focuses on Monica)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (to Chandler) Take me home! (they quickly leave)
Phoebe's place, Phoebe and Mike are there

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey Mikey

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Hey P

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Setting rat traps.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: To kill Bob??

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: No, no, to test his neck strength.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, Mike, I don't want to kill him! I thought we were just gonna capture him and, and you know, set him free in the country side where he can maybe meet a friendly possom and a wisecracking owl.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Ok, ok, I'll throw away the traps.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'll find Bob, I'll get him. Bob? (starts looking) Bob! Robert! (looks at cabinet under sink) Oh wait, I think I hear him. Oh - Oh my god! Bob had babies! Bob's a mom!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Better think of a new name for him.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I don't know, I kinda like Bob for a girl.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: I don't know, I mean I'm not sure (they hear cracking voice from somewhere else in the kitchen, probably a rat caught in a trap) ...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my god, we killed Bob!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Maybe it wasn't Bob, maybe it was a mouse.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Suzie? (Runs over there to check)
Mon and Chan's, Ross enters

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Whazzup??

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Seriously dude, 3 years ago.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Listen, can you do me a favor? I'm gonna be out today. Can you just keep an eye on Joey, make sure nothing happens between him and Molly?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You don't trust him?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wh - No. Some woman who sounded a lot like Joey called earlier and asked for her daughter, the "hot nanny".

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Is this really your long term plan, for me to run interference? Because I could get a job any day now.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You do appear right on the cusp of something. Come on man, I'm sure he'll lose interest in a week or two, but for now can you please just do this for me?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Allright, fine, but don't blame me if it doesn't work. Because you know as well as I do that once Joey sets his mind on something, more often than not, he's going to have sex with it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well we gotta do something, ok? Nannies like her don't grow on trees. (pause)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Picturing that tree?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I am, yes.
Hallway, Joey get out of his appartment and Chandler jumps out of his

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Where you going, Joe?
Joey falls on floor and gets up

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: For a walk.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh. You mind if I join you?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Actually, that will be long. You know, I really need to organize my thoughts.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Your thoughts? Plural?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Allright, fine, I only have one thought! It's about the hot nanny, I gotta see her!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm afraid I can't let you do that, Joe.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Now YOU'RE telling me I can't see her?? You guys are killing me! She's forbidden fruit! It's like ... like she's the princess and I'm the stable boy ... Why are you doing this, huh? Did Ross tell you not to let me go over there?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, as a matter of fact he did, so I can't let you go.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Huh. Interesting. Now there are obstacles. Hot nanny and me against the world. This is the kind of stuff great novels are made of.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Great novels?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Fine ... mediocre porn
Rachel's office, Rachel and Gavin there, phone rings, Gavin picks it up

lines.speaker: Gavin
lines.text: Gavin Mitchelle's office.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Rachel Green's office!! Give me that phone! (takes the phone) Hello, this is Rachel Green, how can I help you? Uh huh ... ok then ... I'll pass you back to your son (gives phone to Gavin)

lines.speaker: Gavin
lines.text: Hey Mom! No, that's just my secretary. (Rachel is upset)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Um, excuse me Gavin, I have a question I need to ask you.

lines.speaker: Gavin
lines.text: Mom, I'll call you later. Yeah. (hangs up) (to Rachel) Yes?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: If you like looking at butts so much why don't you just go look at a mirror?

lines.speaker: Gavin
lines.text: Thank god you finally said that, I saw you make a note on your pad three hours ago. (Rachel throws away that paper) Man, I really bug you, don't I?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, please, I don't care about you enough to bug me. In fact, from now on, I'm going take the high road. And I'm going be very very nice to you, you "momma's boy", starting right now.
door knock, Monica enters

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey Rach!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ready for your birthday lunch?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah I am, I am! Oh, but first of all, Monica, I would like to introduce you to my very talented colleage and more importantly my wonderful friend Gavin Mitchelle.

lines.speaker: Gavin
lines.text: Pleased to meet you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Pleased to meet you. So you're coming to Rachel's party tonight?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh no no no no no, Gavin can't, he already has plans, most likely with his mother.

lines.speaker: Gavin
lines.text: Well I don't mind, I'll cancel. I would never miss my secretary's birthday. (leaves)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Why did you invite him?? I can't stand that guy!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You were just being so nice to him!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I was faking it! Can't you tell when I'm being fake?
A man walks by

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey, Mr Philips, nice suit!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Right there! That was so fake!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Shh!
Rachel's birthday party

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (to Monica) I still can't believe you invited Gavin. Allright, he is the last person I want to see.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, you're welcome for the party. I'm glad you're having a good time.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: God, I hope he doesn't show up. Of course he's not gonna show up, the guy hates me.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Does he?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Maybe he's bothering you so much because he likes you. It's like in first grade when Skippy Langwild always pushed me on the playground because he secretly had a crush on me?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, Monica, you think Skippy liked you? Honey, all those buys had a bet to see if he can knock you over.

lines.speaker: Molly
lines.text: (with Emma in a basinet) She's out, I'm gonna take her home.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, ok, thank you. (Molly leaves) (to Monica) Do you see what all the guys see in her?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wouldn't kick her out of bed. No more Vodka for me! (put her glass down)
Joey comes over

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey Rach, so can I sing happy birthday to you now?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, sure!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh ... (Starts to sing) Happy birth ... (sees Molly leaving) oh, see you later (runs after Molly)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey Rach, somebody got you shoes!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, give me! (opens the box happily, then gets freaked and throws away the box, she and Mon jump up the sofa)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Be careful, be careful! These are my rat babies!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Yeah. We have rat babies now.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ahhhh , you brought rats to my birthday party?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So this is what a stroke feels like.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I had to bring them! We killed their mother, they're our responsibility now. You know, they require constant care. You should know that, Rachel, you're a mother.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Are you comparing my daughter to a rat?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No! Seven rats! I think we should take them home, we need feed them.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: whhh wait, you're gonna leave my party to take care of a box of rats?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well I'm sorry Rachel, but I'm not like you, ok? Not everyone can afford help. (she and Mike leave)
Ross gets out of the bathroom, sees Chandler

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Where the hell are Joey and Molly? I asked you to watch them.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (In a helium voice, holding a balloon) I'm sorry, I got a little occupied.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: We have to stop them before something happens!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (still helium voice) Right behind you, big guy! (they both head for the door)
Ross's place, Molly and Joey are talking on the couch

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So you see, Molly, what people don't understand is that acting is a discipline. It takes a lot of hard work.

lines.speaker: Molly
lines.text: So where did you study?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, I didn't go to college.

lines.speaker: Molly
lines.text: No, where did you study acting?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Molly ... people don't study acting ...
Ross and Chandler walk in, Joey looks disappointed

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Molly, ah, do you mind giving us just a minute?

lines.speaker: Molly
lines.text: Sure, I'll go check on Emma.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thanx.
Molly gets up and walks away

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (upset) Will the stable boy never get the princess??

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What do you think you're gonna do, have sex with her right here on my couch?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No ... the leather sticks to my ass. You know, this isn't fair. What makes you think that I'm just gonna sleep with her and then blow her off? Huh? Can't you guys open your minds to the possibility that I actally like her, and might want something real? (pause) Look, the truth is, I haven't felt this way about anyone since Rachel, ok? I didn't think I could ever love again.
pause

lines.speaker: Ross and Chandler
lines.text: Come on! Joe!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ok (admits)
knock on door, Ross opens, it's a woman

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Hi, is Molly here?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, come on in. Molly?

lines.speaker: Molly
lines.text: (goes out of the room) Hey! Guys, this is Tabatha (they kiss on the lips in a romantic way). (to Ross) I'll see you tomorrow.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ok ... (they leave the room, long pause) Well, uh, Joey, I guess we have no problem.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It's like my favorite fairy tale come true! (Chandler looks at him) The princess, the stable boy and the lesbian!
Phoebe's place, Phoebe and Mike enter

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ok, ok, you start preparing the formula and I start changing the box and then we gotta put them straight to bed.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Hey, when did we become one of those couples whose lives revolve around their rats?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, you know what, they're our responsibility now.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Ok, fine, these rats are our responsibility. What happens when they mate and there are hundreds of them?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Mate? They're all brothers and sisters.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Yeah - not such a problem with rats. No, they're more of a "love the one you're with" kind of animals.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No. (opens the box) wh - get off your sister! Oh my god, what are we gonna do? We have 7 rats. So what if each of them has 7 rats? And then each of those have 7 rats? That's like ... (starts counting with her fingers) That's math I can't even do! What are we gonna do?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Well, I know this is gonna sound crazy, but, we could not let the box of rats ruin our lives.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, ok. I guess you're right. Allright, so we should just give them away. But to nice families, with children, and reduced fat wheat thins. They're Bob's favorites.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: It's gonna be ok.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You must think I'm crazy.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: No, I think you're sweet.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Good. It's just so hard, it's hard for me to ... let them go. I guess it just brings back memories, you know, from ... when I gave birth to my brother's triplets and I had to give them up. (Mike is shocked) I haven't told you about that yet, have I?
Rachel's party, Rachel is on the balcony, Monica goes there also

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi. Thanks for the party, honey. Should I help you clean up?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No way! You had your party, now I have mine! Is everything alright?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, I just get a little bummed when my birthday's over.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, at least you have one thing to be happy about. That jerk Gavin from your office didn't show up (Gavin shows up at the balcony windows).

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Mmm hmm.

lines.speaker: Gavin
lines.text: Yeah, hey. (comes in to the balcony)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, we weren't talking about you. No, no way to recover.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No.
Monica goes back inside

lines.speaker: Gavin
lines.text: Nice party.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, it was, and you would have seen it if you didn't showed up at (looks at his watch) ... 9:30?? God! Oh, this party was lame ...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (from inside) Again, you're welcome.

lines.speaker: Gavin
lines.text: Look, I'll just give you this and go.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, you bought me a present! Why?

lines.speaker: Gavin
lines.text: Well, let me explain how birthday parties usually work. There are presents, and a cake, perhaps a fourth or fifth person. Ok, I ... got you the present to make up for being such a jerk to you earlier.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Aww. Well, ok, well that's very nice. And you wrote a card (opens the card). "From Gavin"

lines.speaker: Gavin
lines.text: I really mean it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (opens the present, it's a green scarf) Awww, awww, it's beautiful.

lines.speaker: Gavin
lines.text: You don't mind? (puts it around her neck) Well, what do you know, it fits!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: See, Gavin, you're capable of being a nice guy. Why did you give me such a hard time?

lines.speaker: Gavin
lines.text: I'm not sure.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well Monica seems to think it's because you have feelings for me.

lines.speaker: Gavin
lines.text: I do have feelings for you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You do?

lines.speaker: Gavin
lines.text: Yes, I feel that you are a little annoying.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: See? Why, Gavin, why? Right when I'm about to change my opinion of you, you go and you ... (he kisses her) and you do that ... (they kiss again)
[cut to Ross from his appartment, holding baby toys and is shocked to see them kissing]
ENDING CREDITS

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (sings in a helium voice) First I was afraid, I was petrified (very happy)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (walks in) Hey

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (normal voice) Hi

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Listen, I think I've left something here.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, well someone left this (shows a green jacket). This is yours?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (likes the jacket) No, but I like it. I think I left one of my rat babies.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, uh, well, I haven't seem it but if I do I'll let you know.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ohmygod! Rat baby! Rat baby! Rat baby! (screams from another room)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (realizes) Ooh, maybe that's him!
